signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: NZD/CHF at 0.7182 (implied) — NZD outperformance dominates G10 as the RBNZ remains in a hiking cycle (2.5% rate) vs SNB on hold at 0.0%. The +250bp carry advantage for NZD is amplified by a 1.448% G10 performance spread. RBNZ 2.5%. CHF remains the G10 laggard as safe-haven demand stays muted with VIX at 16.5.

signals.title: USD/CHF — US CPI Miss + Options Volatility
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.8093 — The pair tumbled 0.70% following a softer-than-expected US June CPI report, triggering a slide in US 2Y yields to 3.728% (-0.75%). Options market pricing shows an elevated 4.8pt Vol Risk Premium (IV 10.5% vs HV30 5.7%), indicating high demand for protection as the pair tests the 0.8100 handle. The bull steepener regime in the US curve (+85.7bp) confirms recession/disinflation pricing, weighing on the USD despite its +375bp carry advantage over CHF.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Options Skew + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.23 — Price remains well above the 160.00 psychological level previously associated with BoJ intervention warnings. COT LF net short -104,231 (29.1% long) shows extreme crowded short JPY positioning, while the 25d Risk Reversal at -1.42 (moderate put skew) signals the options market is paying a premium for USD/JPY downside protection. Elevated IV (7.2%) vs HV30 (3.5%) results in a +3.8pt VRP, reflecting institutional hedging against a sharp carry unwind or BoJ action.

signals.title: AUD/USD — COT vs CESI Divergence + Volatility
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6976 — COT LF net long +24,098 (63.8% long) indicates bullish speculative positioning, yet the AUD Economic Surprise Index (CESI) shows a strong negative bias (-10 net misses). This divergence suggests specs are overextended relative to deteriorating domestic data. IV at 12.9% vs HV30 7.0% creates a significant +5.9pt VRP, the highest in G10, signaling that the options market is pricing in a volatility spike. Watch for a failure to hold 0.7000 to trigger a long-unwind.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + BoC Decision
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4056 — The pair's correlation with WTI Oil has collapsed to -0.900 vs a 252d norm of +0.001 (z = -2.02σ), indicating a total decoupling from energy prices as macro drivers take precedence. Despite WTI rising 2.35% to $80.0, CAD strength is driven by the upcoming BoC meeting on 15 July. USD/CAD remains supported by a +150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage, but the negative CESI for USD (+59 beats) vs neutral CAD data suggests the USD outperformance is likely to be peaking.
